comment:
llm — General-purpose LLM inference client.

 Provides:
   LlmError    — error set for all LLM operations
   LlmConfig   — endpoint, model, timeout, think-mode settings
   LlmClient   — HTTP chat-completion client (OpenAI + Ollama)

 Response post-processing (stripThinkBlock, isMalformedResponse, etc.) lives
 in src/common/llm.zig alongside the string utilities they depend on.
detail:
The `llm` module serves as a comprehensive interface for interacting with large language models (LLMs), specifically designed to abstract the complexities of model inference, configuration, and communication. Its primary purpose is to provide a unified API for developers to leverage LLM capabilities, including error handling, configuration management, and integration with external services like OpenAI and Ollama. The module is structured around key abstractions such as error handling, configuration settings, and HTTP client operations, ensuring seamless interaction with various LLM endpoints.

The module's architecture revolves around three main components: error management, configuration handling, and HTTP communication. The `LlmError` struct defines a standardized set of error types, enabling consistent error reporting across operations. The `LlmConfig` struct encapsulates critical settings such as API endpoints, model selection, timeout values, and think-mode parameters, allowing users to customize their LLM interactions. The `LlmClient` struct manages the HTTP client and integrates with external services, providing methods for initialization, completion handling, and response processing.

Key implementation details include the use of utility functions like `writeEscapedString` for safe string manipulation and the `isOllamaEndpoint` function to validate endpoint types. The module leverages design patterns such as dependency injection for the HTTP client and modular error handling to ensure robustness. The documentation emphasizes the importance of understanding these components to effectively utilize the module's features.

Overall, this module exemplifies a well-structured approach to LLM integration, balancing flexibility with reliability through clear abstractions and comprehensive documentation.

members.name: LlmConfig
members.comment: Defines LLM configuration with methods for model checks and endpoint validation, managing model state and API access.

members.name: isThinkingModel
members.comment: Returns true when thinking is explicitly enabled for this config.

members.name: extractModelName
members.comment: Extract the model name from a model reference like "provider:model:name".

members.name: isOllamaEndpoint
members.comment: Check if an endpoint is an Ollama-native endpoint (uses /api/chat).

members.name: writeEscapedString
members.comment: Writes a null-terminated string to a writer, handling escaped characters properly.

members.name: LlmClient
members.comment: Manages LLM interaction with fixed buffers; owns allocator and config; provides core API methods.

members.name: init
members.signature: fn init(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, config: LlmConfig) -> LlmClient

members.name: complete
members.signature: fn complete(self: *LlmClient, prompt: []const u8, max_tokens: usize, temperature: f32, system: ?[]const u8) -> LlmError!?[]const u8

members.name: postJson
members.signature: fn postJson(self: *LlmClient, url: []const u8, json_body: []const u8) -> LlmError!?[]const u8
members.comment: POST `json_body` to `url` and return the extracted response text.

members.name: available
members.comment: Check whether the LLM endpoint is reachable.

members.name: stripThinkBlockInline
members.comment: Minimal think-block stripper used internally by extractResponseText.

members.name: LlmClient init with v1/chat/completions URL uses it directly
LlmClient init with v1/completions URL normalises to v1/chat/completions
LlmClient init with OpenAI API URL sets is_openai_format
